examples.title: Algorithm: Queue Operations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM QueueOperations
INPUT: queue, an empty queue
OUTPUT: None

BEGIN
 PROCEDURE Enqueue(value)
 IF queue is full THEN
 THROW OverflowError
 ELSE
 IF queue is empty THEN
 SET front to 0
 END IF
 SET rear to rear + 1
 SET queue[rear] to value
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE

 PROCEDURE Dequeue()
 IF queue is empty THEN
 THROW UnderflowError
 ELSE
 SET value to queue[front]
 SET front to front + 1
 IF front > rear THEN
 RESET front and rear to -1
 END IF
 RETURN value
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE

 PROCEDURE Peek()
 IF queue is empty THEN
 THROW UnderflowError
 ELSE
 RETURN queue[front]
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE

 PROCEDURE isEmpty()
 RETURN front == -1 OR front > rear
 END PROCEDURE
END ALGORITHM
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the basic operations of a queue, including enqueue, dequeue, peek, and isEmpty.
